name: Tokyo
pointsTip: ANA round-trip awards from the US start at 75k miles in economy. Book via Virgin Atlantic for better availability.

itinerary.title: Shibuya & Shinjuku Immersion
activities: Cross the iconic Shibuya Scramble and explore Center-gai street
Lunch at Fuunji for their legendary tsukemen dipping ramen
Wander through Meiji Shrine and the forested Yoyogi Park
Evening at Golden Gai in Shinjuku — hop between tiny six-seat bars

itinerary.title: Asakusa, Akihabara & Traditional Tokyo
activities: Morning visit to Senso-ji Temple and Nakamise-dori shopping street
Explore the Akihabara electric district for anime and retro games
Afternoon tea at Yanaka Ginza, a charming old-town shotengai
Dinner omakase at a counter sushi spot in Tsukiji Outer Market

itinerary.title: Harajuku, Omotesando & Roppongi
activities: Stroll Takeshita Street in Harajuku for street fashion and crepes
Window-shop the luxury boutiques along Omotesando-dori
Visit teamLab Borderless digital art museum in Azabudai Hills
Sunset drinks at the Mori Tower observation deck in Roppongi Hills

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Kamakura
activities: Train to Kamakura to see the Great Buddha at Kotoku-in
Hike the Daibutsu Trail through bamboo groves to Zeniarai Benten Shrine
Lunch on Komachi-dori — try shirasu-don (whitebait rice bowl)
Return to Tokyo for a final evening exploring Shimokitazawa's vintage shops

topThingsToDo: Witness the organized chaos of Shibuya Crossing
Eat your way through Tsukiji Outer Market at dawn
Experience a traditional onsen bath house
Get lost in the neon labyrinth of Shinjuku at night
Ride the bullet train to a day-trip destination
description: Tokyo is a city of electrifying contrasts, where ancient temples sit in the shadow of glowing skyscrapers and a convenience store onigiri can rival a Michelin-starred meal. The energy here is relentless yet somehow orderly, pulling you from ramen counters to robot restaurants to silent shrines in a single afternoon.
visaNote: US citizens get 90-day visa-free entry.

name: Bangkok
pointsTip: EVA Air awards via 40k Star Alliance miles one-way. Also check Japan Airlines for great routing through Tokyo.

itinerary.title: Grand Palace & Old Town
activities: Tour the Grand Palace and the Emerald Buddha at Wat Phra Kaew
Walk to Wat Pho to see the massive Reclining Buddha
Cross the Chao Phraya by ferry to explore Wat Arun at golden hour
Dinner at Err Urban Rustic Thai near the river for refined street food

itinerary.title: Markets, Street Food & Chinatown
activities: Morning at Chatuchak Weekend Market — over 15,000 stalls
Lunch at Jeh O Chula for their viral tom yum mama noodle soup
Afternoon exploring Yaowarat Road in Chinatown
Evening street food crawl — try pad thai at Thipsamai and mango sticky rice at Mae Varee

itinerary.title: Modern Bangkok & Nightlife
activities: Brunch at Roast Coffee in Thonglor, Bangkok's trendy neighborhood
Visit MOCA Bangkok or the Jim Thompson House Museum
Sunset cocktails at Octave Rooftop Bar on the 49th floor of the Marriott
Night out on Khao San Road or the speakeasy bars of Ekkamai

topThingsToDo: Feast on $1 pad thai from legendary street stalls
Cruise the Chao Phraya River at sunset
Explore the golden spires of the Grand Palace
Get a world-class Thai massage for under $10
Shop until you drop at Chatuchak Weekend Market
description: Bangkok is sensory overload in the best possible way — incense drifting from golden temples, sizzling woks on every corner, tuk-tuks weaving through traffic, and rooftop bars with skyline views that never end. It's one of the world's great food cities, where a $2 meal on a plastic stool can change your life.
visaNote: US citizens get 30-day visa-free entry, extendable to 60 days.

name: Bali
pointsTip: Singapore Airlines Saver awards at 47.5k KrisFlyer miles round-trip from select US cities via Singapore.

itinerary.title: Ubud & Rice Terraces
activities: Morning walk through the Tegallalang Rice Terraces
Visit the Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary in central Ubud
Lunch at Locavore — one of Bali's most acclaimed restaurants
Evening traditional Kecak fire dance performance at Ubud Palace

itinerary.title: Waterfalls & Temples
activities: Chase waterfalls at Tegenungan and Tibumana Falls
Visit the ancient Tirta Empul water temple for a purification ritual
Afternoon at Gunung Kawi, a stunning rock-cut temple complex
Sunset dinner at Swept Away restaurant overlooking the Ayung River

itinerary.title: Seminyak & Beach Life
activities: Brunch at Sisterfields in Seminyak for excellent coffee and avocado toast
Surf lesson at Kuta or Double Six Beach
Afternoon at Potato Head Beach Club with infinity pool views
Watch the sunset at Tanah Lot sea temple perched on a rocky outcrop

itinerary.title: Nusa Penida Day Trip
activities: Fast boat to Nusa Penida island
Hike to the dramatic Kelingking Beach cliff viewpoint
Snorkel with manta rays at Manta Point
Visit the natural infinity pool at Angel's Billabong before returning

topThingsToDo: Sunrise hike up Mount Batur volcano
Surf the breaks at Uluwatu or Canggu
Swim under a hidden jungle waterfall
Take a Balinese cooking class in Ubud
Snorkel with manta rays off Nusa Penida
description: Bali casts a spell that brings people back year after year — emerald rice terraces cascading down volcanic hillsides, incense-filled temples draped in marigolds, and sunsets over the Indian Ocean that stop you mid-sentence. Whether you're chasing waves in Canggu or finding stillness in an Ubud yoga studio, this island delivers.
visaNote: US citizens can get a 30-day visa on arrival for $35 USD, extendable once.

name: Seoul
pointsTip: Korean Air SkyPass awards at 80k miles round-trip from the US. Transfer from Chase Ultimate Rewards.

itinerary.title: Palaces & Traditional Seoul
activities: Tour Gyeongbokgung Palace during the Changing of the Guard ceremony
Rent a hanbok to walk through the Bukchon Hanok Village alleyways
Lunch at Tosokchon for their famous samgyetang (ginseng chicken soup)
Stroll along Cheonggyecheon Stream as it lights up at dusk

itinerary.title: Hongdae, K-Culture & Street Food
activities: Explore the Hongdae district for indie shops, murals, and busking performances
Visit the HYBE Insight museum for K-pop history
Afternoon at Gwangjang Market — try bindaetteok (mung bean pancakes) and mayak gimbap
Evening at a Korean BBQ restaurant in Mapo-gu — Maple Tree House is iconic

itinerary.title: Gangnam, Shopping & Nightlife
activities: Morning hike up Namsan Tower for panoramic city views
Shop the underground malls at COEX in Gangnam and visit Starfield Library
Afternoon in the trendy Garosu-gil neighborhood for cafes and boutiques
Night out in Itaewon or a Korean pojangmacha (street food tent bar)

topThingsToDo: Feast at Gwangjang Market, Seoul's oldest traditional market
Hike Bukhansan National Park on the city's doorstep
Experience a traditional Korean jjimjilbang spa
Explore the DMZ on a half-day tour from the city
description: Seoul is where 600-year-old palaces and K-pop billboards share the same skyline, creating one of Asia's most dynamic capitals. The food alone is worth the trip — from sizzling barbecue joints that stay open until 4 AM to grandmothers ladling perfect kimchi jjigae in century-old markets.
visaNote: US citizens get 90-day visa-free entry with K-ETA registration.

name: Hanoi
pointsTip: ANA round-trip awards from the US at 75k miles. Route through Tokyo Narita for a free stopover.

itinerary.title: Old Quarter & Street Food
activities: Wander the 36 ancient streets of the Old Quarter, each named for its trade
Eat pho bo at Pho Gia Truyen on Bat Dan Street — the city's most famous bowl
Visit Hoan Kiem Lake and the Ngoc Son Temple on its tiny island
Evening street food tour — bun cha, banh mi, and egg coffee at Giang Cafe

itinerary.title: History & Culture
activities: Tour the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex and the Presidential Palace
Visit the Temple of Literature, Vietnam's first university from 1070
Lunch at Bun Bo Nam Bo for their namesake beef noodle salad
Afternoon at the Vietnamese Women's Museum, then a water puppet show at Thang Long Theatre

itinerary.title: Ha Long Bay Day Trip
activities: Early drive to Ha Long Bay (or take a seaplane for stunning aerial views)
Cruise through limestone karsts and emerald waters on a traditional junk boat
Kayak through hidden caves and floating fishing villages
Swim and enjoy a fresh seafood lunch on board before returning to Hanoi

topThingsToDo: Eat the world's best pho for under $2
Cruise the otherworldly Ha Long Bay
Sip egg coffee at a tiny Old Quarter cafe
Ride a motorbike through the chaotic streets (or take a Grab)
description: Hanoi is a city that moves to its own rhythm — motorbikes weave through tree-lined French colonial boulevards, vendors balance entire kitchens on shoulder poles, and the aroma of pho simmering since before dawn fills every alley. It's raw, chaotic, and utterly captivating.
visaNote: US citizens need an e-visa ($25, valid 90 days single entry) — apply online before departure.

name: Singapore
pointsTip: Singapore Airlines Saver awards at 60k KrisFlyer miles round-trip. Transfer from Chase, Amex, or Citi.

itinerary.title: Marina Bay & Gardens
activities: Morning at Gardens by the Bay — walk the OCBC Skyway between the Supertrees
Visit the Cloud Forest dome with its indoor waterfall
Lunch at Satay by the Bay hawker center
Evening light show at the Marina Bay Sands waterfront, then drinks at CÉ LA VI rooftop

itinerary.title: Hawker Culture & Neighborhoods
activities: Breakfast at Tiong Bahru Market for chwee kueh and kaya toast
Explore the colorful Peranakan shophouses of Katong and Joo Chiat
Lunch at Maxwell Food Centre — try the legendary Tian Tian chicken rice
Afternoon in Little India and Kampong Glam for Arab Street textiles and Sultan Mosque

itinerary.title: Sentosa & Chinatown
activities: Morning at Chinatown Heritage Centre and the Buddha Tooth Relic Temple
Ferry to Sentosa Island for beaches and the S.E.A. Aquarium
Afternoon exploring the National Gallery Singapore in the former Supreme Court building
Farewell dinner at Burnt Ends for world-class modern barbecue

topThingsToDo: Eat your way through UNESCO-recognized hawker centres
Watch the Supertree light show at Gardens by the Bay
Swim in the Marina Bay Sands infinity pool (hotel guests only)
Explore the futuristic Jewel Changi Airport waterfall
description: Singapore packs an extraordinary punch for its tiny size — Michelin-starred hawker stalls, futuristic gardens, pristine streets, and a food culture that might be the best on Earth. It's the rare city where you can eat a $3 plate of chicken rice and a $300 tasting menu on the same day and both will blow your mind.
visaNote: US citizens get 90-day visa-free entry.

name: Kyoto
pointsTip: Fly into Osaka KIX — JAL awards from 60k miles round-trip via partner programs. Kyoto is 75 min by train.

itinerary.title: Eastern Kyoto Temples
activities: Early morning at Fushimi Inari shrine — walk the thousands of vermillion torii gates before crowds arrive
Visit Kiyomizu-dera Temple and its dramatic wooden terrace
Lunch in the Gion district — try yudofu (hot tofu) at Okutan, serving since 1635
Evening stroll through Gion hoping to spot a geiko heading to an engagement

itinerary.title: Golden Pavilion & Arashiyama
activities: Morning at Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion) reflected in its mirror pond
Take the scenic Sagano Romantic Train to Arashiyama
Walk through the towering Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
Visit the Iwatayama Monkey Park for hilltop views and wild macaques

itinerary.title: Zen Gardens & Nishiki Market
activities: Meditate at Ryoan-ji and contemplate its enigmatic rock garden
Explore Nishiki Market — the 'Kitchen of Kyoto' — for pickles, matcha, and tamagoyaki
Afternoon tea ceremony experience at Camellia Garden near Kodai-ji Temple
Farewell kaiseki dinner at Gion Nanba for an exquisite multi-course meal

topThingsToDo: Walk thousands of torii gates at Fushimi Inari at sunrise
Experience a traditional multi-course kaiseki dinner
Stroll the ethereal Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
Attend a tea ceremony in a historic machiya townhouse
description: Kyoto is Japan distilled to its essence — over 2,000 temples and shrines, geisha districts preserved in amber, and seasonal beauty so profound it inspired an entire aesthetic philosophy. In spring, cherry blossoms drape the Philosopher's Path in pink; in autumn, maple leaves set the hillside temples ablaze.
visaNote: US citizens get 90-day visa-free entry.

name: Taipei
pointsTip: EVA Air Kitty Jet awards from 60k Star Alliance miles round-trip. Excellent premium cabin product.

itinerary.title: Temples, Tea & Night Markets
activities: Morning at Longshan Temple, one of Taipei's oldest and most ornate
Explore the hip Dadaocheng neighborhood and its preserved Baroque-style buildings
Afternoon tea tasting at a traditional teahouse in Maokong, reached by gondola
Night feast at Shilin Night Market — try stinky tofu, pepper buns, and bubble tea

itinerary.title: Taipei 101 & Modern City
activities: Morning hike up Elephant Mountain for the classic Taipei 101 skyline photo
Visit the National Palace Museum for its unparalleled Chinese art collection
Lunch at Din Tai Fung original branch for soup dumplings
Afternoon exploring Ximending pedestrian district and the Red House creative market

itinerary.title: Jiufen & North Coast
activities: Day trip to Jiufen, the hillside village that inspired Spirited Away
Wander the narrow lantern-lit alleys and sip taro ball soup
Side trip to the otherworldly Yehliu Geopark and its mushroom rock formations
Return for a final night market crawl at Raohe Street Night Market

topThingsToDo: Eat your way through Taipei's legendary night markets
Ride the gondola to Maokong for hillside tea plantations
Hike Elephant Mountain for Taipei 101 skyline views
Day trip to the misty lantern-lit streets of Jiufen
description: Taipei is the most underrated food capital in Asia — a city where night markets serve michelin-worthy meals for $3, convenience stores stock gourmet bentos, and the original Din Tai Fung still has lines around the block. Beyond the food, it offers hot springs, lush mountain hikes, and some of the friendliest locals you'll ever meet.
visaNote: US citizens get 90-day visa-free entry.

name: Chiang Mai
pointsTip: Fly Bangkok to Chiang Mai on Thai Smile from 5k Star Alliance miles one-way after getting to Bangkok on a partner award.

itinerary.title: Old City Temples
activities: Visit Wat Chedi Luang, a massive 14th-century temple ruin in the Old City
Explore Wat Phra Singh and its exquisite Lanna murals
Lunch at SP Chicken near Chiang Mai Gate for the best roast chicken in town
Join a monk chat session at Wat Suan Dok to learn about Buddhist practice

itinerary.title: Doi Suthep & Markets
activities: Morning drive up to Wat Phra That Doi Suthep for mountaintop temple views
Visit the Hmong hill tribe village near the summit
Afternoon Thai cooking class at Mama Noi or Pantawan Cooking School
Evening at the massive Saturday or Sunday Walking Street night market

itinerary.title: Nature & Elephants
activities: Full-day visit to Elephant Nature Park — an ethical rescue sanctuary
Feed, walk alongside, and bathe rescued elephants
Lunch at the sanctuary with other visitors
Return for a traditional Lanna khantoke dinner with dance performance

topThingsToDo: Take a Thai cooking class with market shopping
Visit an ethical elephant sanctuary
Explore the moat-encircled Old City and its 30+ temples
Shop the legendary Sunday Walking Street night market
description: Chiang Mai is the gentle soul of Thailand — a moat-ringed old city filled with golden temples, surrounded by misty mountains and elephant sanctuaries. It's where digital nomads sip $2 lattes in gorgeous cafes, night markets stretch for miles, and a cooking class can teach you more about Thai culture than any museum.
visaNote: US citizens get 30-day visa-free entry, extendable to 60 days.

name: Jaipur
pointsTip: Air India awards via Star Alliance at 80k miles round-trip. Also check Emirates via Dubai for 90k Skywards miles.

itinerary.title: The Pink City's Landmarks
activities: Start at Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds) — photograph its 953 honeycomb windows
Tour the City Palace complex, still home to the Jaipur royal family
Lunch at LMB (Laxmi Mishthan Bhandar) for rajasthani thali since 1727
Explore Jantar Mantar, the world's largest stone astronomical observatory

itinerary.title: Amber Fort & Surroundings
activities: Morning jeep ride up to Amber Fort, a stunning hilltop palace-fortress
Explore the Sheesh Mahal (Mirror Palace) with its thousands of inlaid mirrors
Lunch at 1135 AD restaurant inside the fort for regal dining
Afternoon visit to the Jal Mahal (Water Palace) floating in Man Sagar Lake

itinerary.title: Markets & Hidden Gems
activities: Morning at Nahargarh Fort for panoramic views of the pink city below
Shopping in Johari Bazaar for gems, textiles, and block-printed fabrics
Lunch of dal baati churma at Chokhi Dhani, an ethnic village resort
Sunset drinks at Bar Palladio, an opulently decorated Italian-Indian bar

topThingsToDo: Wander through the rose-pink streets of the walled old city
Explore the majestic Amber Fort
Shop for block-printed textiles and gemstones in the bazaars
Watch the sunset from Nahargarh Fort overlooking the city
description: Jaipur paints everything in shades of terracotta and gold — from its iconic pink-walled Old City to the amber sandstone of its hilltop forts. It's India at its most theatrical, where elephants still climb fortress ramps, bazaars overflow with gems and silk, and every palace tells a story of Rajput valor and artistry.
visaNote: US citizens need an e-visa — apply online at least 4 days before departure ($25 for 30 days).

name: Maldives
pointsTip: Hyatt points go far here — Park Hyatt Maldives at 30k World of Hyatt points/night. Transfer from Chase Ultimate Rewards.

itinerary.title: Arrival & Overwater Bliss
activities: Scenic seaplane transfer from Malé to your resort atoll
Settle into your overwater villa and swim from your private deck
Afternoon snorkel session on the house reef — spot reef sharks and turtles
Sunset dinner on a sandbank set up by the resort staff

itinerary.title: Ocean Adventures
activities: Morning dolphin-watching cruise in the atoll lagoon
Guided snorkel trip to a manta ray cleaning station
Afternoon spa treatment in an overwater treatment room
Night snorkel or dive to see bioluminescent plankton on the beach

itinerary.title: Island Exploration
activities: Sunrise yoga on the beach
Visit a local inhabited island to see Maldivian village life and taste hedhikaa snacks
Afternoon diving or semi-submarine coral reef tour
Farewell underwater restaurant dinner at Ithaa or Sea (depending on resort)

topThingsToDo: Sleep in an overwater villa above crystal-clear lagoons
Snorkel with manta rays and whale sharks
Dine at an underwater restaurant surrounded by marine life
Watch bioluminescent plankton glow on the beach at night
description: The Maldives is the closest thing to paradise that exists on Earth — 1,192 coral islands scattered across the Indian Ocean like emeralds on blue silk. Overwater villas hover above lagoons so clear you can watch stingrays glide beneath your glass floor, and the reefs below teem with more color than a painter's palette.
visaNote: All nationalities get a free 30-day visa on arrival.

name: Colombo
pointsTip: Qatar Airways awards from 70k Avios round-trip via Doha. Transfer from Chase, Amex, or Capital One.

itinerary.title: Colombo City Discovery
activities: Morning at Gangaramaya Temple, an eclectic Buddhist temple on Beira Lake
Walk through Pettah Market, the city's chaotic and colorful bazaar district
Lunch at Ministry of Crab in the old Dutch Hospital for Sri Lankan crab curry
Sunset stroll along Galle Face Green promenade with street food vendors

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Galle
activities: Train along the coast to Galle — one of the world's most scenic rail rides
Explore the 16th-century Galle Fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Lunch at Lucky Fort Restaurant for fresh seafood in the fort walls
Browse boutiques and galleries in the fort's atmospheric narrow streets

itinerary.title: Tea Country & Culture
activities: Early train to Kandy through lush hill country and tea plantations
Visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic, Sri Lanka's most sacred Buddhist site
Tour a working tea factory and taste fresh Ceylon tea at the source
Return to Colombo for a farewell rice and curry feast at Upali's

topThingsToDo: Ride the scenic coastal and hill country trains
Explore the colonial Galle Fort at sunset
Feast on fiery Sri Lankan rice and curry
Visit ancient Buddhist temples and ruins
description: Sri Lanka packs a continent's worth of experiences into an island the size of West Virginia — ancient Buddhist kingdoms, misty tea plantations, golden beaches, and some of the most generous curries in South Asia. Colombo itself has transformed into a cosmopolitan food scene while keeping its chaotic charm intact.
visaNote: US citizens need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) — apply online before departure ($50).

name: Paris
pointsTip: Air France Flying Blue Promo Rewards often drop to 25k miles one-way. Transfer from Chase, Amex, Citi, or Capital One.

itinerary.title: Iconic Paris
activities: Morning at the Louvre — head straight to Winged Victory and the Dutch Masters to avoid Mona Lisa crowds
Walk through the Tuileries Garden to Place de la Concorde
Lunch at Café de Flore in Saint-Germain-des-Prés
Sunset at the Eiffel Tower — book the summit ticket in advance

itinerary.title: Montmartre & Le Marais
activities: Morning climbing to Sacré-Cœur for panoramic views from Montmartre
Coffee and croissant at the pink La Maison Rose
Afternoon exploring Le Marais — falafel at L'As du Fallafel on Rue des Rosiers
Evening Seine river cruise watching the monuments light up

itinerary.title: Versailles Day Trip
activities: RER train to the Palace of Versailles — arrive at opening for the Hall of Mirrors
Wander the formal gardens and Marie Antoinette's hamlet
Lunch at La Petite Venise in the gardens
Return for a farewell dinner in the bistros of the 11th arrondissement — try Le Bistrot Paul Bert

topThingsToDo: Watch the Eiffel Tower sparkle at night from Trocadéro
Stroll the Seine at golden hour past Notre-Dame
Get lost in the Louvre (and actually enjoy it with a strategy)
Eat croissants, cheese, and wine in every arrondissement
Explore Versailles' impossibly grand palace and gardens
description: Paris needs no introduction, yet it still surprises — a corner boulangerie with the world's best croissant, a hidden courtyard in the Marais that takes your breath away, golden light on limestone facades that makes you understand why artists have been obsessed with this city for centuries.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Barcelona
pointsTip: Iberia Avios flash sales drop to 17k one-way from the US East Coast. Transfer from Chase, Amex, or Capital One.

itinerary.title: Gaudí & Gothic Quarter
activities: Morning timed entry to La Sagrada Familia — book the tower access for aerial views
Walk through the enchanting Park Güell with its mosaic terraces
Lunch at Bar Cañete near Las Ramblas for exceptional Catalan tapas
Afternoon wandering the Gothic Quarter — find the Barcelona Cathedral and hidden Plaça Reial

itinerary.title: Beach, Barceloneta & El Born
activities: Morning at Barceloneta Beach with a swim in the Mediterranean
Brunch at La Mar Salada for fresh seafood near the port
Explore the Picasso Museum in the El Born neighborhood
Evening tapas crawl on Carrer Blai in Poble-sec — pintxos for €1 each

itinerary.title: Montjuïc & Eixample
activities: Cable car up to Montjuïc Castle for harbor panoramas
Visit the Fundació Joan Miró gallery
Lunch at Cervecería Catalana in the Eixample for the city's best patatas bravas
Tour Casa Batlló at twilight when Gaudí's facade glows with colored light

topThingsToDo: Stand inside the kaleidoscopic nave of La Sagrada Familia
Eat tapas until you can't move in Poble-sec
Watch the sunset over the city from Park Güell's terrace
Swim in the Mediterranean at Barceloneta Beach
Explore Gaudí's fantastical Casa Batlló and Casa Milà
description: Barcelona is where Gaudí's surrealist architecture meets Mediterranean beach culture, where late-night tapas crawls spill into 3 AM dance floors, and where every turn reveals another jaw-dropping facade. It's sun-soaked, art-drunk, and unapologetically fun.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Lisbon
pointsTip: TAP Air Portugal via 40k Star Alliance miles one-way. Also check Aeroplan for great Europe awards.

itinerary.title: Alfama & Belém
activities: Morning getting lost in Alfama's narrow cobblestone alleys and tiled facades
Visit São Jorge Castle for panoramic views over the red rooftops
Tram 28 ride through the historic neighborhoods
Afternoon in Belém — pastéis de nata at Pastéis de Belém and the Jerónimos Monastery

itinerary.title: Bairro Alto & Time Out Market
activities: Morning at the LX Factory creative complex for brunch, art, and vintage shopping
Walk across the 25 de Abril Bridge viewpoint at Miradouro da Graça
Lunch at Time Out Market Lisbon for curated stalls from the city's best chefs
Sunset drinks at a Bairro Alto rooftop bar, then fado music at Clube de Fado in Alfama

itinerary.title: Sintra Day Trip
activities: Train to Sintra to explore the candy-colored Pena Palace on its forested hilltop
Hike to the mysterious Initiation Well at Quinta da Regaleira
Lunch at Tascantiga in Sintra's charming old town
Return to Lisbon for a farewell dinner of bacalhau at Cervejaria Ramiro

topThingsToDo: Eat pastéis de nata warm from the oven at the original bakery
Listen to hauntingly beautiful fado in an Alfama tavern
Ride the iconic yellow Tram 28 through the hills
Explore the fairy-tale palaces of Sintra
description: Lisbon glows — literally. The city's famous golden light bathes tiled facades, terracotta rooftops, and the Tagus River in a warmth that makes every photo look like a painting. Add soulful fado music drifting from tiny taverns, custard tarts fresh from century-old ovens, and wine that costs less than water, and you have Europe's coolest capital.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Prague
pointsTip: United awards at 30k Saver miles one-way from the US. Also check Aeroplan for partner routings.

itinerary.title: Old Town & Castle District
activities: Watch the Astronomical Clock strike the hour in Old Town Square
Cross the Charles Bridge early morning before the crowds arrive
Tour Prague Castle and St. Vitus Cathedral, the world's largest ancient castle complex
Lunch at Lokál Dlouhá for classic Czech cuisine and fresh tank beer

itinerary.title: Jewish Quarter & Malá Strana
activities: Morning at the Jewish Quarter — visit the Old Jewish Cemetery and Spanish Synagogue
Walk through the picturesque Malá Strana neighborhood below the castle
Lunch at Café Savoy for their legendary open-face sandwiches
Afternoon at the Lennon Wall and Petřín Hill for a hilltop panorama via funicular

itinerary.title: Beer Culture & Hidden Prague
activities: Morning exploring Vyšehrad fortress with river views and the Slavín cemetery
Craft beer tour at Letná Beer Garden and Prague Beer Museum
Lunch at Kantýna for excellent Czech charcuterie and steak
Evening classical concert inside one of Prague's baroque churches

topThingsToDo: Walk the Charles Bridge at sunrise with the castle glowing behind it
Drink the world's best pilsner for $2 in a medieval beer hall
Tour the largest ancient castle complex on Earth
Catch a classical concert in a stunning baroque church
description: Prague is a Gothic fairy tale come to life — a thousand spires piercing the morning mist, a castle the size of a small town watching over cobblestone streets, and beer halls where $2 buys a pint of the world's finest pilsner. It offers the grandeur of Paris at a fraction of the price.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Dubrovnik
pointsTip: United excursionist perk lets you add Dubrovnik as a free stopover on a multi-city Europe award.

itinerary.title: Old Town Walls & Stradun
activities: Walk the full circuit of Dubrovnik's medieval city walls — a 2km loop with Adriatic views
Stroll the marble-paved Stradun main street and visit the Rector's Palace
Lunch at Nishta in the Old Town for creative vegetarian cuisine in a 14th-century building
Sunset drinks at Buža Bar, a cliffside bar carved into the outer walls above the sea

itinerary.title: Lokrum Island & Beaches
activities: Ferry to Lokrum Island — swim in the Dead Sea saltwater lake and explore the botanical gardens
Afternoon at Banje Beach with the Old Town as your backdrop
Kayak around the Old Town walls at golden hour
Dinner at Proto for fresh Adriatic seafood on a candlelit terrace

itinerary.title: Cable Car & Wine Country
activities: Cable car up Mount Srđ for breathtaking views of the old town and islands
Day trip to the Pelješac Peninsula for wine tasting — try the local Dingač red
Stop at Ston for fresh oysters pulled from the bay
Return for a farewell walk along the Lazareti strip at night

topThingsToDo: Walk the full medieval city wall circuit above the Adriatic
Drink cocktails at Buža Bar perched on the cliffs
Kayak beneath the ancient city walls at sunset
Day trip to Lokrum Island for swimming and peacock-spotting
description: Dubrovnik's terracotta rooftops and limestone walls rise dramatically from the Adriatic Sea, creating one of Europe's most photogenic cities. Walking the ancient walls with the turquoise sea shimmering below and islands dotting the horizon is a genuine bucket-list moment — Game of Thrones fans will recognize every corner.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Santorini
pointsTip: Fly to Athens on 40k Star Alliance miles, then catch a budget Aegean/Olympic Air hop to Santorini.

itinerary.title: Oia & Caldera Views
activities: Settle into Oia and explore its blue-domed churches and narrow marble paths
Visit the Maritime Museum for the island's seafaring history
Lunch at Ammoudi Bay — climb down 300 steps for grilled octopus by the water
Watch the world-famous Oia sunset from the castle ruins

itinerary.title: Wine, Beaches & Akrotiri
activities: Morning at Akrotiri, the 'Minoan Pompeii' — a Bronze Age city preserved under volcanic ash
Visit Red Beach and the dramatic volcanic cliffs
Afternoon wine tasting at Santo Wines or Venetsanos Winery with caldera views
Dinner in Fira at Koukoumavlos for creative Greek tasting menus

itinerary.title: Sailing & Village Life
activities: Catamaran sail around the caldera — swim in hot springs and snorkel volcanic reefs
BBQ lunch on board with local wine
Explore the quiet village of Pyrgos and its medieval Venetian castle
Farewell dinner at Selene in Fira for Santorini's finest modern Greek cuisine

topThingsToDo: Watch the sunset in Oia — one of the most famous views on Earth
Sail the caldera and swim in volcanic hot springs
Taste Assyrtiko wine at a cliffside winery
Walk the cliffside path from Fira to Oia
description: Santorini looks impossibly beautiful — white-washed buildings cascading down volcanic cliffs into a deep blue caldera, with sunsets that paint the sky in shades no filter can replicate. It's the Greek island that launched a million Instagram posts, but experiencing it in person still takes your breath away.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Amsterdam
pointsTip: Delta SkyMiles flash sales to AMS frequently drop to 26k miles round-trip. Also check KLM Flying Blue promos.

itinerary.title: Canals, Museums & Jordaan
activities: Morning timed-entry visit to the Anne Frank House (book weeks in advance)
Walk the canal ring through the picturesque Jordaan neighborhood
Lunch at The Pancake Bakery for Dutch-style pannenkoeken
Afternoon at the Rijksmuseum — see Rembrandt's Night Watch and Vermeer's Milkmaid

itinerary.title: Van Gogh, Markets & Nightlife
activities: Morning at the Van Gogh Museum on Museumplein
Browse the Albert Cuyp Market in De Pijp for stroopwafels and raw herring
Afternoon biking along the Amstel River — rent from MacBike
Evening exploring hidden brown cafes (bruine kroegen) like Café 't Smalle

itinerary.title: Vondelpark & Hidden Gems
activities: Morning stroll through Vondelpark with coffee from Lot Sixty One
Visit the NDSM Wharf for street art, creative studios, and waterfront vibes
Lunch at Foodhallen in Oud-West for gourmet market bites
Afternoon canal cruise through the UNESCO-listed canal ring

topThingsToDo: Bike along the canal ring like a local
Stand before Rembrandt's Night Watch at the Rijksmuseum
Visit the Anne Frank House for a deeply moving experience
Eat fresh stroopwafels from a street market
description: Amsterdam's charm is in its contradictions — it's a world-class city that feels like a village, where you can cycle from a Rembrandt masterpiece to a cutting-edge design studio in ten minutes along a 17th-century canal. The golden-age townhouses leaning over the water, the bike bells, the warm glow of brown cafes — it's effortlessly cool.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Rome
pointsTip: ITA Airways frequently has business class sales. Also check United at 30k Saver miles one-way.

itinerary.title: Ancient Rome
activities: Morning at the Colosseum with arena floor access (book in advance)
Walk through the Roman Forum and up Palatine Hill
Lunch at Roscioli for their legendary cacio e pepe and curated wine list
Afternoon tossing a coin in the Trevi Fountain and climbing the Spanish Steps

itinerary.title: Vatican & Trastevere
activities: Early entry tour of the Vatican Museums and Sistine Chapel
Climb St. Peter's Basilica dome for city-wide views
Cross the Tiber for lunch in Trastevere at Da Enzo al 29 (arrive early — no reservations)
Evening passeggiata through the cobblestone streets with gelato from Fatamorgana

itinerary.title: Piazzas & Neighborhoods
activities: Morning coffee at Sant'Eustachio Il Caffè, Rome's most celebrated espresso bar
Explore the Pantheon and lively Piazza Navona
Lunch at Testaccio Market for supplì and trapizzino
Sunset aperitivo overlooking the city from Pincian Hill in Villa Borghese

topThingsToDo: Stand inside the Colosseum and imagine gladiator combat
Marvel at the Sistine Chapel ceiling up close
Eat cacio e pepe, carbonara, and supplì in every neighborhood
Throw a coin in the Trevi Fountain at sunrise before the crowds
Wander Trastevere's cobblestone streets at golden hour
description: Rome is 3,000 years of history served with a side of the world's best pasta. You'll round a corner and stumble upon a 2,000-year-old temple, eat carbonara that ruins all future carbonara, and realize that la dolce vita isn't just a movie — it's a daily practice here.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Edinburgh
pointsTip: British Airways Avios at 13k one-way off-peak from select US cities. Transfer from Chase or Amex.

itinerary.title: Old Town & Royal Mile
activities: Tour Edinburgh Castle perched on its volcanic rock
Walk the Royal Mile from the Castle to the Palace of Holyroodhouse
Lunch at The Witchery for Scottish fine dining in Gothic surroundings
Descend into the underground vaults beneath the Old Town on a guided tour

itinerary.title: Arthur's Seat & New Town
activities: Morning hike up Arthur's Seat for 360-degree city panoramas
Explore the Georgian splendor of New Town and its independent shops on Thistle Street
Lunch at The Scran & Scallie in Stockbridge for elevated Scottish pub food
Afternoon at the Scottish National Gallery on the Mound

itinerary.title: Whisky & Leith
activities: Scotch whisky tasting experience at the Scotch Whisky Experience on the Royal Mile
Explore the vibrant Leith neighborhood and its waterfront restaurants
Lunch at The Kitchin in Leith — Michelin-starred Scottish seasonal cuisine
Evening ghost tour through the atmospheric closes and graveyards of Old Town

topThingsToDo: Hike Arthur's Seat for volcano-top city views
Explore Edinburgh Castle and its crown jewels
Tour a Scotch whisky distillery
Visit during the Fringe Festival in August for the world's largest arts festival
description: Edinburgh is a city built on drama — volcanic crags, a medieval castle brooding over the skyline, cobblestone closes hiding centuries of secrets, and the wild green hill of Arthur's Seat rising right from the city center. In August, the Fringe Festival transforms it into the planet's greatest stage.
visaNote: US citizens get 6 months visa-free entry to the UK.

name: Budapest
pointsTip: LOT Polish Airlines via Warsaw often has great Star Alliance awards at 30k miles one-way.

itinerary.title: Buda Side & Castle District
activities: Morning at Buda Castle and the Hungarian National Gallery
Walk through the Fisherman's Bastion for fairy-tale Danube views
Visit the stunning Matthias Church with its colorful tiled roof
Cross the Chain Bridge to Pest for dinner at Borkonyha Winekitchen

itinerary.title: Thermal Baths & Jewish Quarter
activities: Morning soak at Széchenyi Thermal Bath — Europe's largest medicinal bath complex
Walk through Heroes' Square and City Park
Afternoon exploring the Jewish Quarter's ruin bars — start at Szimpla Kert
Dinner at Mazel Tov for Middle Eastern food in a gorgeous courtyard

itinerary.title: Parliament & the Danube
activities: Guided tour of the Hungarian Parliament — one of Europe's most spectacular buildings
Pay respects at Shoes on the Danube Bank, the powerful Holocaust memorial
Lunch at the Great Market Hall for lángos and Hungarian paprika products
Evening Danube river cruise watching Parliament and the castle light up

topThingsToDo: Soak in the ornate Széchenyi or Gellért thermal baths
Bar-hop through the legendary ruin bars of the Jewish Quarter
Watch the Danube light up Parliament and the Castle at night
Tour the breathtaking Hungarian Parliament building
description: Budapest is two cities in one — hilly Buda with its castle and thermal springs, and flat Pest with its ruin bars and grand boulevards — joined by the Danube and some of Europe's most beautiful bridges. Soak in a century-old thermal bath by day, crawl the ruin bars by night, and spend less than you would in almost any Western European capital.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free in the Schengen Area.

name: Mexico City
pointsTip: Aeromexico via SkyTeam at 25k miles round-trip. Also ultra-low-cost carriers like Volaris from US border cities.

itinerary.title: Centro Histórico & Coyoacán
activities: Morning at the Zócalo — visit the Metropolitan Cathedral and the Templo Mayor Aztec ruins
Walk through the Palacio de Bellas Artes for its stunning murals by Rivera and Orozco
Lunch at El Cardenal for classic Mexican breakfast/lunch since 1969
Afternoon at the Frida Kahlo Museum (Casa Azul) in Coyoacán — book tickets online

itinerary.title: Roma-Condesa & Markets
activities: Brunch at Lalo! in Roma Norte for inventive Mexican breakfast
Explore the tree-lined streets and art deco architecture of Condesa
Afternoon at Mercado de San Juan for exotic meats and gourmet ingredients
Dinner at Contramar for their iconic red-and-green grilled fish tostadas

itinerary.title: Teotihuacán & Xochimilco
activities: Early morning drive to climb the Pyramids of Teotihuacán — the ancient city of the gods
Walk the Avenue of the Dead and ascend the Pyramid of the Sun
Return for late lunch tacos al pastor at El Vilsito (a mechanic shop by day)
Afternoon floating the colorful trajineras of Xochimilco canals with micheladas

topThingsToDo: Eat tacos al pastor from a trompo spinning over charcoal
Climb the Pyramid of the Sun at Teotihuacán
Visit Frida Kahlo's Casa Azul in Coyoacán
Explore the murals of Diego Rivera across the city
Float the ancient canals of Xochimilco
description: Mexico City is a megalopolis with the soul of a village — Aztec pyramids beneath colonial cathedrals, taco stands that outperform fine dining, and a creative energy that's making it one of the world's most exciting cities right now. It's overwhelming, addictive, and criminally affordable.
visaNote: US citizens get 180 days visa-free.

name: Tulum
pointsTip: Fly into Cancún (CUN) — Southwest Rapid Rewards often has availability at 15-20k points one-way.

itinerary.title: Ruins & Beach
activities: Morning visit to the Tulum Archaeological Site — Mayan ruins perched on a Caribbean cliff
Swim at the beach directly below the ruins
Lunch at Burrito Amor in Tulum Pueblo for incredible $3 burritos
Afternoon relaxing on the powdery white sand of Playa Paraíso

itinerary.title: Cenotes & Jungle
activities: Swim in the crystal-clear Cenote Suytun with its beam of light hitting the water
Explore Gran Cenote for snorkeling with turtles in an underground cave
Lunch at Hartwood for wood-fired farm-to-table cuisine in an open-air jungle restaurant
Afternoon bike ride along the beach road, stopping at boutique hotels and art galleries

itinerary.title: Sian Ka'an Biosphere
activities: Day trip to Sian Ka'an Biosphere Reserve — a UNESCO-protected coastal wetland
Float down the ancient Mayan canal system through mangroves
Spot dolphins, manatees, and tropical birds from a boat
Return for a farewell sunset dinner at Kin Toh treehouse restaurant

topThingsToDo: Swim in a sunlit cenote underground cave
Visit Mayan ruins overlooking the Caribbean Sea
Float through the Sian Ka'an Biosphere Reserve
Snorkel with sea turtles at Gran Cenote
description: Tulum is where ancient Mayan mysticism meets bohemian beach culture — clifftop ruins gazing over turquoise waters, jungle cenotes glowing like liquid sapphire, and a hotel zone that feels like a curated Instagram dream. It's the Riviera Maya at its most photogenic and wellness-obsessed.
visaNote: US citizens get 180 days visa-free.

name: Vancouver
pointsTip: Alaska Mileage Plan is king for Pacific Northwest flights — 12.5k miles one-way from the West Coast.

itinerary.title: Stanley Park & Gastown
activities: Bike or walk the Stanley Park Seawall — a 10km loop with mountain and ocean views
Visit the Vancouver Aquarium and the totem poles at Brockton Point
Lunch in Gastown at Tacofino for West Coast tacos
Explore the cobblestone streets of Gastown and watch the Steam Clock whistle

itinerary.title: Granville Island & Mountains
activities: Morning at Granville Island Public Market for smoked salmon and fresh pastries
Take the Sea-to-Sky Gondola in Squamish for jaw-dropping coastal mountain views
Afternoon at the Capilano Suspension Bridge swaying 70m above the rainforest
Dinner at Vij's for celebrated Indian-Canadian fusion cuisine

itinerary.title: Grouse Mountain & Neighborhoods
activities: Morning hike up the Grouse Grind — 2,830 steps up the North Shore mountains
Explore the diverse food scene on Commercial Drive and Main Street
Lunch at Miku for aburi (flame-seared) sushi — some of the best outside Japan
Sunset at English Bay Beach watching the mountains glow pink

topThingsToDo: Bike the Stanley Park Seawall with mountains as your backdrop
Eat world-class sushi rivaling Tokyo's best
Hike the Grouse Grind for North Shore mountain views
Browse Granville Island's artisan market
description: Vancouver is nature's city — snow-capped mountains plunge into the Pacific Ocean, old-growth rainforests border the downtown core, and you can ski and surf in the same day. Add one of North America's most diverse food scenes (the sushi here rivals Tokyo) and you've got a city that punches far above its weight.

name: Banff
pointsTip: Fly into Calgary (YYC) — WestJet or Air Canada from 15k Aeroplan miles one-way. Banff is 90 min drive.

itinerary.title: Lake Louise & Moraine Lake
activities: Early morning drive to Moraine Lake to see the iconic turquoise water before crowds (shuttle required in summer)
Hike the Lake Louise Lakeshore Trail to the Plain of Six Glaciers teahouse
Lunch at the Fairmont Chateau Lake Louise with a lakeside view
Return to Banff for dinner at Park Distillery with Rocky Mountain cuisine

itinerary.title: Icefields Parkway Drive
activities: Drive the Icefields Parkway (Highway 93N) — one of the world's most scenic roads
Stop at Peyto Lake for its distinctive wolf-head-shaped turquoise waters
Walk on the Columbia Icefield glacier via the Skywalk
Photograph the weeping wall and Mistaya Canyon along the route

itinerary.title: Banff Town & Gondola
activities: Ride the Banff Gondola up Sulphur Mountain for 360-degree Rocky Mountain views
Soak in the Banff Upper Hot Springs on the mountainside
Lunch at The Bison for locally sourced Alberta beef and bison
Afternoon hike to Johnston Canyon's ink pots and frozen waterfalls

topThingsToDo: See the impossibly turquoise water of Moraine Lake at sunrise
Drive the legendary Icefields Parkway
Hike to a backcountry teahouse at Lake Louise
Soak in hot springs with mountain views
description: Banff is the Canadian Rockies at their most spectacular — lakes so turquoise they look photoshopped, glaciers that cascade into wildflower meadows, and elk wandering through a charming mountain town. Whether you're skiing in winter or hiking in summer, the scenery here is genuinely the most dramatic in North America.

name: Havana
pointsTip: JetBlue TrueBlue points from 8k one-way from Fort Lauderdale. Southwest also flies from select cities.

itinerary.title: Old Havana & the Malecón
activities: Wander through Habana Vieja — the UNESCO-listed colonial old town
Visit Plaza de la Catedral and the ornate baroque Catedral de La Habana
Lunch at La Guarida, Cuba's most famous paladar in a crumbling mansion
Sunset stroll along the Malecón seawall with rum and live music

itinerary.title: Classic Cars, Cigars & Rum
activities: Vintage car tour through Vedado in a 1957 Chevy convertible
Visit a cigar factory or the Partagás shop to watch master torcedores at work
Rum tasting at the Havana Club museum in San Francisco de Paula
Evening at Fábrica de Arte Cubano, an art gallery-concert venue-nightclub hybrid

itinerary.title: Revolution History & Fusterlandia
activities: Tour the Plaza de la Revolución and the Che Guevara murals
Visit the Museo de la Revolución in the former Presidential Palace
Explore Fusterlandia — artist José Fuster's mosaic-covered neighborhood in Jaimanitas
Farewell mojito at La Bodeguita del Medio, Hemingway's favorite bar

topThingsToDo: Ride in a colorful 1950s American vintage convertible
Sip mojitos at Hemingway's favorite bar
Photograph the crumbling-yet-beautiful colonial architecture
Experience live salsa music at an open-air venue
description: Havana is a time capsule wrapped in a salsa rhythm — candy-colored 1950s Chevys cruise past crumbling colonial mansions, live son music spills from every doorway, and the Malecón at sunset is one of the world's great urban scenes. It's romantic, gritty, and unlike anywhere else on Earth.
visaNote: US citizens need a Cuban tourist card (visa) and must travel under an approved OFAC category such as 'Support for the Cuban People.'

name: San Juan
pointsTip: No passport needed — it's a US territory! Southwest, JetBlue, and Spirit all fly direct. 10-15k points one-way.

itinerary.title: Old San Juan
activities: Walk the blue cobblestone streets past pastel colonial buildings
Tour Castillo San Felipe del Morro, the 16th-century Spanish fortress
Lunch at La Casita Blanca in Santurce for authentic mofongo and pernil
Afternoon exploring San Cristóbal Fort and flying kites on the fortress lawn

itinerary.title: Beaches & Rainforest
activities: Morning at Condado Beach for swimming and sunbathing
Drive to El Yunque National Rainforest — the only tropical rainforest in the US system
Hike to La Mina Falls and swim in the pool beneath the cascade
Return for dinner and cocktails at La Factoría, one of the world's best bars

itinerary.title: Bioluminescent Bay & Culture
activities: Morning exploring the street art of Santurce with a local guide
Lunch at Jungle Bird in Santurce for craft cocktails and small plates
Afternoon at the Museo de Arte de Puerto Rico
Evening kayak tour to a bioluminescent bay — paddle through water that glows electric blue

topThingsToDo: Explore the colorful colonial streets of Old San Juan
Hike to waterfalls in El Yunque National Rainforest
Kayak through a bioluminescent bay at night
Tour the massive El Morro fortress
description: San Juan delivers Caribbean beauty without the passport hassle — 500 years of Spanish colonial history painted in every shade of pastel, world-class cocktail bars tucked behind unmarked doors, and rainforest waterfalls just 45 minutes from the beach. It's affordable, accessible, and criminally underrated.

name: Buenos Aires
pointsTip: Aerolíneas Argentinas via SkyTeam partners. Also LATAM via British Airways Avios at 60k round-trip.

itinerary.title: San Telmo & La Boca
activities: Morning at the San Telmo antique market on Calle Defensa
Visit the colorful Caminito street in La Boca with its painted tin houses
Lunch at El Obrero, a beloved neighborhood bodegón in La Boca since 1954
Afternoon tango lesson at a milonga in San Telmo

itinerary.title: Recoleta & Palermo
activities: Morning at the Recoleta Cemetery — find Evita's tomb among the ornate mausoleums
Visit MALBA (Museum of Latin American Art) in Palermo
Lunch at Don Julio for what many call the world's best steak — arrive early
Explore Palermo Soho's boutiques, street art, and cocktail bars

itinerary.title: Tango, Wine & Microcentro
activities: Walk the grand Avenida de Mayo from the Casa Rosada to Congress
Coffee at Café Tortoni, the city's oldest cafe (since 1858)
Afternoon wine tasting of Argentine Malbecs at Pain et Vin or Aldo's
Evening tango show at Café de los Angelitos or Rojo Tango

topThingsToDo: Eat the world's best steak with a glass of Malbec
Watch (or dance) tango in a smoky San Telmo milonga
Wander the Recoleta Cemetery's ornate mausoleums
Browse San Telmo's Sunday antique market
description: Buenos Aires is the Paris of South America — wide Haussmann-style boulevards, a cafe culture that treats sitting for hours as an art form, and a passion for tango, steak, and Malbec that borders on religion. Dinner starts at 10 PM, nightclubs open at 2 AM, and the city's creative energy never sleeps.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Cartagena
pointsTip: JetBlue TrueBlue from 15k points one-way from Fort Lauderdale. Also Spirit and Avianca have cheap cash fares.

itinerary.title: Walled City & Getsemaní
activities: Walk through the walled Old City — bougainvillea-draped balconies on every corner
Visit the Palacio de la Inquisición and the Plaza de Bolívar
Lunch at La Cevichería for fresh ceviche (Bourdain's favorite)
Evening in Getsemaní neighborhood for street art, salsa bars, and Café Havana

itinerary.title: Rosario Islands & Beach
activities: Boat to the Rosario Islands for snorkeling in clear Caribbean waters
Beach day on Isla Barú with fresh fish lunch and coconut cocktails
Visit the Oceanario aquarium on Isla San Martín de Pajarales
Return for dinner at Carmen restaurant for modern Colombian tasting menu

itinerary.title: Castillo & Culture
activities: Morning tour of Castillo San Felipe de Barajas, the largest Spanish fortress in the Americas
Explore the modern Bocagrande neighborhood and its beach
Lunch of cazuela de mariscos (seafood stew) at a local fonda
Sunset cocktails on a rooftop in the walled city watching the skyline glow

topThingsToDo: Get lost in the candy-colored walled city streets
Dance salsa at Café Havana in Getsemaní
Boat to the Rosario Islands for Caribbean snorkeling
Explore the massive Castillo San Felipe fortress
description: Cartagena is the Caribbean at its most seductive — a 16th-century walled city painted in every color of the sunset, where horse-drawn carriages clip-clop past fruit vendors balancing baskets on their heads and salsa music pulses from open doorways until dawn.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Cusco
pointsTip: LATAM via British Airways Avios at 50k round-trip to Lima. Budget carriers fly Lima to Cusco from $40.

itinerary.title: Cusco Historic Center (acclimate!)
activities: Take it slow on day one — you're at 3,400m (11,150ft) elevation
Walk the Plaza de Armas and visit the Qorikancha (Inca Temple of the Sun)
Drink coca tea to help with altitude and explore the San Pedro Market
Gentle evening at Limbus rooftop bar with pisco sours and city views

itinerary.title: Sacred Valley
activities: Day trip to the Sacred Valley — start at Pisac's Inca ruins and artisan market
Visit the circular Inca agricultural terraces of Moray
Explore the Maras salt mines — thousands of salt ponds cascading down a hillside
Dinner at Mil, Virgilio Martínez's restaurant at 3,568m serving hyper-local Andean cuisine

itinerary.title: Machu Picchu
activities: Early train from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes (book PeruRail or IncaRail)
Bus up to Machu Picchu for sunrise — enter through the Sun Gate
Guided tour of the citadel, Intihuatana stone, and Temple of the Sun
Optional Huayna Picchu climb for aerial views of the ruins below

itinerary.title: Rainbow Mountain or Cusco Exploration
activities: Option A: Hike to Vinicunca (Rainbow Mountain) at 5,200m — stunning striped geology
Option B: Explore Cusco's lesser-known sites — Sacsayhuamán fortress and the bohemian San Blas quarter
Lunch at Chicha by Gastón Acurio for modern Peruvian in a colonial courtyard
Farewell dinner at MAP Café inside the Pre-Columbian Art Museum

topThingsToDo: Stand atop Machu Picchu as the clouds part at sunrise
Explore the Sacred Valley's Inca ruins and salt mines
Taste modern Andean cuisine at world-class restaurants
Hike to the surreal Rainbow Mountain
description: Cusco was the navel of the Inca Empire, and its magic endures — cobblestone streets built atop Inca stone walls, cloud forests hiding lost citadels, and a food scene that's transforming Andean ingredients into world-class cuisine. Machu Picchu is the headline act, but the Sacred Valley and the city itself are equally unforgettable.
visaNote: US citizens get 183 days visa-free.

name: Rio de Janeiro
pointsTip: LATAM Pass awards from 60k miles round-trip. Also check Avianca LifeMiles for Star Alliance partners.

itinerary.title: Christ the Redeemer & Santa Teresa
activities: Morning cog railway up Corcovado to Christ the Redeemer — arrive early for clear views
Explore the bohemian Santa Teresa neighborhood and its street art
Lunch at Bar do Mineiro for feijoada — Brazil's iconic black bean and pork stew
Walk the colorful Selarón Steps connecting Lapa to Santa Teresa

itinerary.title: Sugarloaf & Beaches
activities: Cable car to the top of Sugarloaf Mountain for panoramic bay views
Spend the afternoon on Copacabana and Ipanema beaches
Fresh açaí bowl and agua de coco from a beach vendor
Sunset at Arpoador Rock watching the sun dip behind Dois Irmãos mountains

itinerary.title: Lapa & Local Culture
activities: Morning at the Botanical Garden (Jardim Botânico) — 140 acres of tropical plants
Lunch at Olympe for refined Brazilian cuisine by Claude Troisgros
Afternoon exploring the Museu de Arte do Rio (MAR)
Night out in Lapa — samba clubs, caipirinhas, and live music at Rio Scenarium

topThingsToDo: Stand at the feet of Christ the Redeemer atop Corcovado
Watch the sunset from Arpoador Rock between Copacabana and Ipanema
Dance samba in the streets of Lapa on a Friday night
Ride the cable car to the summit of Sugarloaf Mountain
description: Rio de Janeiro is nature showing off — granite peaks erupting from tropical forest, golden beaches curving between dramatic headlands, and a giant Christ statue surveying it all with open arms. The city pulses with samba, caipirinhas flow freely, and Cariocas treat every sunset like a celebration.
visaNote: US citizens need an e-visa — apply online before departure ($80, valid for up to 5 years).

name: Medellín
pointsTip: Spirit Airlines often has rock-bottom fares from Fort Lauderdale. Also Avianca via 30k LifeMiles round-trip.

itinerary.title: El Poblado & City Center
activities: Morning at Plaza Botero to see Fernando Botero's oversized bronze sculptures
Ride the MetroCable gondola up to Santo Domingo for hillside community views
Lunch at Mondongo's for bandeja paisa — Colombia's hearty national platter
Explore the Museo de Antioquia for more Botero works and Colombian art

itinerary.title: Comuna 13 & Coffee
activities: Join a graffiti tour of Comuna 13 — once the most dangerous neighborhood, now a vibrant open-air gallery
Ride the outdoor escalators that transformed the community
Afternoon Colombian coffee tasting at Pergamino Café in El Poblado
Evening food and nightlife along Parque Lleras

itinerary.title: Guatapé Day Trip
activities: Day trip to Guatapé — climb the 740 steps of El Peñol rock for breathtaking reservoir views
Explore the colorful zócalos (painted base panels) on Guatapé's charming main street
Lunch of trout fresh from the reservoir at a lakeside restaurant
Return to Medellín for a farewell dinner at El Cielo for avant-garde Colombian cuisine

topThingsToDo: Take the graffiti tour of the transformed Comuna 13
Climb the 740 steps of El Peñol rock in Guatapé
Ride the MetroCable gondola for valley views
Experience Medellín's legendary nightlife in El Poblado
description: Medellín's transformation from one of the world's most dangerous cities to one of its most innovative is nothing short of extraordinary. The City of Eternal Spring sits in a lush valley with year-round 75°F weather, a thriving food and nightlife scene, and a creative energy powered by resilience and reinvention.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Marrakech
pointsTip: Royal Air Maroc joined Oneworld — book via Alaska or British Airways from 25k Avios one-way.

itinerary.title: Medina & Souks
activities: Plunge into the labyrinthine souks from Jemaa el-Fnaa square
Visit the Ben Youssef Madrasa, a masterpiece of Islamic architecture
Lunch at Nomad for modern Moroccan cuisine with rooftop terrace views
Return to Jemaa el-Fnaa at dusk for the nightly spectacle of storytellers, musicians, and food stalls

itinerary.title: Gardens & Palaces
activities: Morning at Jardin Majorelle — Yves Saint Laurent's cobalt blue botanical garden
Visit the adjacent YSL Museum Marrakech
Explore the Bahia Palace and its stunning zellige tilework
Afternoon hammam experience at a traditional bathhouse like Heritage Spa

itinerary.title: Atlas Mountains Day Trip
activities: Drive into the High Atlas Mountains to the Ourika Valley
Hike to the Setti Fatma waterfalls with a local Berber guide
Lunch at a Berber village home — tagine cooked over coals
Return for a farewell dinner at Al Fassia, a women-run Moroccan fine dining institution

topThingsToDo: Get lost (literally) in the medina's ancient souks
Watch Jemaa el-Fnaa come alive at sunset
Stay in a gorgeous traditional riad with a courtyard fountain
Hike the Atlas Mountains with Berber guides
description: Marrakech assaults the senses in the most magnificent way — the call to prayer echoing over terracotta rooftops, the smell of cumin and cedar in the souks, the kaleidoscopic tilework of ancient madrasas, and the theatrical chaos of Jemaa el-Fnaa square where North Africa puts on its nightly show.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Cape Town
pointsTip: Delta SkyMiles to CPT via Johannesburg from 60k miles round-trip. Also check United's South Africa routes.

itinerary.title: Table Mountain & Waterfront
activities: Morning cable car (or hike!) up Table Mountain for one of the world's great views
Explore the V&A Waterfront and the Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa
Lunch at the Neighbourgoods Market in Woodstock on Saturdays
Sunset at Signal Hill overlooking the Atlantic coastline

itinerary.title: Cape Peninsula Drive
activities: Scenic drive along Chapman's Peak — one of the world's most dramatic coastal roads
Visit the penguin colony at Boulders Beach in Simon's Town
Drive to Cape Point and hike to the lighthouse at the edge of the continent
Return via Kalk Bay for fish and chips at Kalky's on the harbor

itinerary.title: Winelands & Bo-Kaap
activities: Morning walk through the colorful Bo-Kaap neighborhood and its Cape Malay heritage
Day trip to Stellenbosch and Franschhoek wine valleys
Lunch at Babylonstoren wine estate with farm-to-table dining
Wine tasting at Delaire Graff for Cabernet Sauvignon with mountain views

topThingsToDo: Summit Table Mountain for a flat-top view of two oceans
Drive the Chapman's Peak coastal road
Visit the penguins at Boulders Beach
Taste world-class wines in Stellenbosch and Franschhoek
Walk the colorful streets of Bo-Kaap
description: Cape Town might be the most dramatically beautiful city on Earth — a flat-topped mountain rising from the sea, penguins waddling on white-sand beaches, vineyards climbing into the mountains, and two oceans colliding at the continent's tip. The food and wine scene rivals Europe at half the price.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free.

name: Zanzibar
pointsTip: Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa from 60k Star Alliance miles round-trip. Great stopover option.

itinerary.title: Stone Town Heritage
activities: Explore the UNESCO-listed Stone Town — a maze of narrow alleys and carved wooden doors
Visit the House of Wonders and the Old Fort
Lunch at Lukmaan Restaurant for authentic Zanzibari biryani and pilau
Evening at Forodhani Gardens night market for Zanzibar pizza and grilled seafood

itinerary.title: Spice Tour & Beaches
activities: Morning spice farm tour — taste clove, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg fresh from the trees
Head to the east coast beaches — Nungwi or Paje for powder-white sand
Afternoon kitesurfing at Paje Beach (Zanzibar is a world-class spot)
Sunset dhow sailing cruise off the coast

itinerary.title: Prison Island & Snorkeling
activities: Boat to Prison Island to meet the giant Aldabra tortoises (some over 100 years old)
Snorkeling trip to Mnemba Atoll — coral gardens with dolphins and tropical fish
Lunch at The Rock Restaurant, perched on a tiny rock in the Indian Ocean
Farewell seafood dinner at Emerson Spice rooftop with Stone Town views

topThingsToDo: Wander the atmospheric alleyways of Stone Town
Snorkel the pristine reefs of Mnemba Atoll
Eat at The Rock Restaurant in the middle of the ocean
Take a spice farm tour and taste fresh tropical spices
description: Zanzibar is the spice island of your daydreams — Stone Town's labyrinth of ancient coral-stone buildings opens onto beaches so white they hurt your eyes, with turquoise water stretching to the horizon. The air smells of cloves and grilled seafood, dhows dot the sunset, and the pace of life slows to an island crawl.
visaNote: US citizens need a visa on arrival ($50) or an e-visa applied in advance.

name: Nairobi
pointsTip: Kenya Airways via Amsterdam on 60k Flying Blue miles round-trip. Transfer from Chase, Amex, or Citi.

itinerary.title: Nairobi National Park & Elephants
activities: Early morning game drive in Nairobi National Park — lions, rhinos, and giraffes with the city skyline behind them
Visit the David Sheldrick Elephant Trust during the 11AM orphan feeding
Lunch at Carnivore Restaurant for their famous game meat nyama choma
Afternoon at the Giraffe Centre in Langata to hand-feed endangered Rothschild's giraffes

itinerary.title: Culture & Food Scene
activities: Morning at the Karen Blixen Museum (Out of Africa) in the leafy Karen neighborhood
Explore the vibrant Maasai Market for beadwork, carvings, and textiles
Lunch at Mama Oliech for the city's best fried tilapia and ugali
Evening at Alchemist Bar in Westlands for Nairobi's buzzing nightlife scene

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Lake Naivasha or the Rift Valley
activities: Drive to Lake Naivasha in the Great Rift Valley
Boat ride among hippos and fish eagles on the lake
Hike in Hell's Gate National Park — bike or walk among zebras and giraffes
Return to Nairobi for a farewell dinner at Talisman in Karen

topThingsToDo: Hand-feed giraffes at the Giraffe Centre
See lions against the Nairobi skyline in Nairobi National Park
Adopt a baby elephant at the Sheldrick Trust
Use Nairobi as a gateway to the Masai Mara safari
description: Nairobi is the only capital city in the world with a national park inside its borders — you can watch lions hunt with skyscrapers in the background. Beyond the safari gateway, it's a booming tech hub with a food scene that spans nyama choma joints to innovative fine dining, and the energy of an African metropolis on the rise.
visaNote: US citizens need an e-visa — apply at least 3 days before departure ($51).

name: Sydney
pointsTip: Qantas awards from 60k points one-way in economy. Also check ANA for 55k miles round-trip on partner carriers.

itinerary.title: Harbour Icons
activities: Walk across the Sydney Harbour Bridge (or do the BridgeClimb for the full experience)
Tour the Sydney Opera House — book a backstage access pass
Lunch at Quay overlooking the harbour for Peter Gilmore's acclaimed cuisine
Ferry to Manly Beach for afternoon surfing and the Corso promenade

itinerary.title: Bondi to Coogee & Neighborhoods
activities: Walk the famous Bondi to Coogee coastal walk — 6km of dramatic cliffside paths
Brunch at Bills in Surry Hills for the ricotta hotcakes that made Bill Granger famous
Explore the Rocks district and its weekend artisan markets
Sunset drinks at Opera Bar with the harbour bridge and Opera House glowing above

itinerary.title: Blue Mountains Day Trip
activities: Train to the Blue Mountains — 90 minutes from the city
Hike to the Three Sisters rock formation at Echo Point
Ride the world's steepest railway at Scenic World through ancient rainforest
Bushwalk through eucalyptus forests and waterfalls before returning to Sydney

topThingsToDo: Walk (or climb) the Sydney Harbour Bridge
Surf at Bondi Beach and walk the coastal trail to Coogee
See a performance inside the Sydney Opera House
Day trip to the Blue Mountains for bushwalking
Eat world-class brunch — Sydney invented modern brunch culture
description: Sydney is the city that has it all — a glittering harbour framed by the world's most recognizable opera house and bridge, golden beaches minutes from the CBD, and a laid-back outdoor lifestyle that makes every other city feel like it's trying too hard. The brunch scene alone is worth the long flight.
visaNote: US citizens need an ETA (Electronic Travel Authority) — apply online before departure ($20 AUD).

name: Queenstown
pointsTip: Air New Zealand awards from 40k Aeroplan miles one-way. Also check United at 40k Saver miles via Auckland.

itinerary.title: Lake Wakatipu & Town
activities: Ride the Skyline Gondola for panoramic views of the Remarkables mountain range
Luge down the mountain on the Skyline track
Lunch at Fergburger — the legendary burger joint with a permanent queue
Afternoon cruise on the vintage TSS Earnslaw steamship across Lake Wakatipu

itinerary.title: Adventure Day
activities: Morning bungee jump at the original Kawarau Bridge (or Nevis for the extreme version)
Jet boat ride through the narrow Shotover River canyons
Afternoon skydiving over the lake and mountains (or scenic helicopter flight)
Relaxing evening at Onsen Hot Pools with mountain views

itinerary.title: Milford Sound Day Trip
activities: Drive or fly to Milford Sound through some of New Zealand's most dramatic scenery
Cruise the full length of the fiord past waterfalls, rainforest, and seal colonies
Watch for dolphins and Fiordland crested penguins
Return via the Mirror Lakes and Eglinton Valley

topThingsToDo: Bungee jump at the birthplace of commercial bungee
Cruise the majestic Milford Sound fiord
Ski the Remarkables or Coronet Peak in winter
Skydive over the lake with mountain panoramas
description: Queenstown is the adventure capital of the world, perched on the shores of a glacial lake with jagged mountains rising on every side. It's where bungee jumping was invented, jet boats were perfected, and the scenery is so dramatic that Peter Jackson chose it for Middle-earth. Come for the adrenaline, stay for the pinot noir.
visaNote: US citizens get 90 days visa-free with an NZeTA ($12 NZD, apply before departure).

name: Fiji
pointsTip: Fiji Airways partners with Alaska and Qantas. Use 55k Alaska miles round-trip from LAX. Also check Wyndham points for resorts.

itinerary.title: Arrival & Island Welcome
activities: Arrive in Nadi and transfer to the Mamanuca or Yasawa Islands by boat or seaplane
Traditional kava welcome ceremony at your resort or village
Afternoon snorkel on the house reef — Fiji has some of the world's best soft coral
Sunset dinner on the beach with fresh kokoda (Fijian ceviche)

itinerary.title: Island Hopping & Marine Life
activities: Island-hopping boat tour to the Mamanuca chain
Snorkel or dive the Rainbow Reef — known as the 'Soft Coral Capital of the World'
Visit Cloud 9, a floating bar and pizzeria in the middle of the ocean
Afternoon village visit to learn about Fijian culture and traditions

itinerary.title: Waterfalls & Relaxation
activities: If on Viti Levu: hike to the Tavoro Waterfalls in Bouma National Heritage Park on Taveuni
Traditional Fijian lovo feast — food cooked underground in earth ovens
Afternoon kayaking through mangrove channels
Final evening beach bonfire under the Southern Cross stars

topThingsToDo: Snorkel the soft coral capital of the world
Experience a traditional kava ceremony
Island-hop between the Mamanuca and Yasawa chains
Relax on beaches that define tropical paradise
description: Fiji is 333 islands of pure tropical bliss — warm turquoise waters teeming with the world's most colorful soft coral, palm-fringed beaches that look computer-generated, and a culture built on the word 'bula' and the genuine warmth behind it. Fijians are consistently ranked among the friendliest people on Earth, and five minutes here you'll understand why.
visaNote: US citizens get 4 months visa-free on arrival.
